## What kind of contribution is this?

<!-- Pick one or more: -->
<!-- - Critique / Challenge -->
<!-- - Case Study -->
<!-- - Reference or Connection -->
<!-- - Question -->
<!-- - Missing Perspective -->
<!-- - Correction -->
<!-- - Other -->

## Which part of the framework does this relate to?

<!-- Link to the specific document(s) if possible, or describe the area: -->
<!-- e.g. "The philosophical foundation argument about logos-narrowing" -->
<!-- e.g. "The where-it-fails analysis — I have a case study that fits" -->
<!-- e.g. "The whole approach — I think there's a fundamental problem" -->

## Your contribution

<!-- Describe your critique, suggestion, case study, question or reference here. -->
<!-- Plain language is perfect. Academic citations welcome but not required. -->
<!-- If you're offering a case study, the format in 1-theory/02-landscape/05-case-studies/ gives a sense of what's useful: summary, why it matters, key patterns, open questions. But don't let the format stop you — a rough description is better than nothing. -->

## Your background (optional)

<!-- Not required, but helpful context. What perspective are you coming from? -->
<!-- e.g. "I'm a teacher and I see coordination failure in education daily" -->
<!-- e.g. "I study game theory and I think the Axelrod framing is oversimplified" -->
<!-- e.g. "I'm a Buddhist practitioner and the contemplative dimension resonates" -->
